title: USSR GNP by Sector of Origin (CIA estimate, 1982 rubles)
notes: CIA/JEC reconstruction of Soviet GNP by sector (industry, construction, agriculture, transport, communications, trade, services, military personnel, other), billion 1982 rubles at factor cost. Comparator-country-only chart, no Iran data -- no existing chart_id concept in CHART_REGISTRY.csv covers this (WDI has no USSR/pre-1991-RUS coverage at all). Keep methodologically separate from narkhoz_national_economy_index_1913_1989 (official Soviet statistics) below -- the two series are known to diverge, see README.

title: USSR GNP by End Use — Consumption, Investment, Government (CIA estimate, 1982 rubles)
notes: Same source/table family as ussr_russia_cia_gnp_by_sector_1950_1987 but by end-use (consumption/investment/other government incl. defense) rather than by sector of origin -- distinct chart concept, kept separate per this project's sector-vs-end-use convention used elsewhere for GDP breakdowns.

title: USSR Consumption Per Capita & Population (CIA estimate)
notes: CIA reconstruction of Soviet consumer-goods/consumer-services/total consumption per capita (1982 established-price rubles) plus USSR population time series. No Iran data; comparator context for living-standards comparisons.

title: USSR GNP Growth & Defense-Spending Burden — CIA Assessment Timeline
notes: Long-format timeline of CIA-cited GNP growth rates, defense-share-of-GNP figures, and defense-spending growth rates, each row citing its originating CIA/JEC document and approximate page -- includes the explicit Soviet-official-vs-CIA growth comparison for 1981-85/1986/1987. Not a single clean numeric series (mixed vintages/definitions per row) so charting this requires picking a sub-slice by indicator; documented as a timeline/citation table, directly analogous to this project's existing CIA Iran assessment narrative entries.

title: Soviet GNP as % of US GNP (CIA geometric-mean estimate)
notes: 6-point series (1960,65,70,75,80,83) from the CIA's own published 'Figure 1' bar chart. Geometric-mean-of-ruble-and-dollar-valuation methodology -- NOT directly comparable to ussr_us_gnp_ruble_valuation_1960_1981 below, which uses ruble valuation only and shows a materially lower ratio; see README methodological note.

title: USSR vs US Total GNP, Ruble Valuation (CIA estimate, 1970 rubles)
notes: USSR and US total GNP in a single (ruble) price system, billion 1970 rubles, plus USSR/US ratio -- from a different, lower-resolution scanned CIA source (DOC_0000498181) than the geometric-mean Figure 1 series. US-side values independently sanity-checked against known US recession years (1970, 1974-75, 1980), all of which show up correctly as dips.

title: USSR vs US Defense Spending, Ruble Valuation (CIA estimate, 1970 rubles)
notes: Same Table 5 source as ussr_us_gnp_ruble_valuation_1960_1981. Shows the well-documented Cold War crossover where Soviet defense spending (ruble valuation) exceeds the US from the early 1970s onward (ratio >100%). One cell (USSR 1966) is blank -- illegible on the source scan, not guessed.

title: USSR Official National-Economy Development Indices (Narodnoe khozyaistvo SSSR, multiple bases)
notes: OFFICIAL SOVIET STATISTICS (as opposed to the ussr_russia_cia_* CIA-reconstruction entries above) -- national income, industrial output (by group A/B), agricultural output, capital investment, labor productivity, etc., as index numbers on several overlapping base years drawn from 4 different yearbook editions (1956/65/75/89). The single richest table (1975 ed., base 1913=1) spans the full Stalin-to-Brezhnev era in one consistent series. This is the pre-1991 command-economy comparator this task brief specifically called out as a gap; no existing chart_id in CHART_REGISTRY.csv is remotely comparable.

title: USSR Population, Urban/Rural (official Soviet statistics)
notes: Official Soviet population figures (total/urban/rural) for 5 benchmark years including two 1913 rows under different border definitions, as printed in the 1956 yearbook.

title: USSR Grain & Technical-Crop Harvest Index (official Soviet statistics)
notes: Gross harvest index (wheat, corn, sunflower, sugar beet, raw cotton, flax), base 1950=100, official Soviet statistics.

title: USSR Livestock Product Output Index (official Soviet statistics)
notes: Meat/milk/wool/egg output index, base 1950=100, official Soviet statistics.

title: USSR Livestock Headcount — Cattle, Pigs, Sheep & Goats (official Soviet statistics)
notes: Million head, all farm categories, mostly annual. Directly documents the 1930-33 collectivization-era livestock collapse (cattle 50.6M in 1930 to 33.5M in 1933) from the Soviet Union's own later official statistics -- a historically significant, source-attributed data point on a contested episode, presented per this project's neutrality policy (official figure, not an advocacy-outlet claim).

title: USSR First Five-Year Plan Agricultural Machinery Output (1928/29-1932 plan target)
notes: Seeders/cultivators/harrows/binders/combines/threshers production, actuals through 1931 plus the 1932 plan target, from the contemporaneous 1932 'Narodnoe khoziaistvo SSSR' journal (not the later fixed yearbook series) -- directly responds to this task brief's call for 'Five-Year Plan targets/actuals.'

title: Imperial Russia Population by Region (Tsarist-era official statistics)
notes: PRE-SOVIET baseline -- population of the Russian Empire by region (European Russia, Vistula/Congress-Poland, Caucasus, Siberia, Central Asia, Finland) at 4 benchmark dates (1858 tax revision, 1897 census, 1904 and 1910 administrative estimates). Genuinely rare pre-1917 comparator data per this task brief; do not confuse with the Soviet-era narkhoz_population_1913_1956 entry above -- different statistical apparatus, different territory definitions in places.

title: Imperial Russia vs. Other Great Powers, Population (Tsarist-era official statistics)
notes: The Russian Empire's own published international population ranking vs. USA/Germany/Austria-Hungary/UK/France/Italy and 8 smaller European states plus Japan, at 2 benchmark dates.

title: Imperial Russia vs. Other Great Powers, Population Density (Tsarist-era official statistics)
notes: Persons per square verst, same country set as the population-comparison entry -- illustrates Russia's low density despite its large total population, a recurring theme in the source yearbooks' own narrative text.

title: Imperial Russia Total Foreign Trade (Tsarist-era official statistics)
notes: Total imports/exports in rubles, 1897-1902 (annual, printed grand totals) and 1908 (by European/Finnish/Asian border section, summed to an all-borders total by us -- flagged in the CSV's notes column since no single printed grand-total cell was captured).
